sections.content:
Angle Types and Rules

Angles are measured in degrees (°). Key angle types:
• Acute: less than 90°
• Right: exactly 90°
• Obtuse: between 90° and 180°
• Straight: exactly 180°
• Reflex: between 180° and 360°

Important angle rules:
• Angles on a straight line add to 180°
• Angles at a point (full rotation) add to 360°
• Vertically opposite angles are equal
• Complementary angles add to 90°; supplementary angles add to 180°

🔑 Parallel lines rules (when a transversal crosses parallel lines):
• Co-interior (same-side interior) angles add to 180° (they're supplementary)
• Alternate angles are equal (Z-angles)
• Corresponding angles are equal (F-angles)

Triangles

The three interior angles of any triangle always add to 180°.

Types of triangles:
• Equilateral: all 3 sides equal, all angles = 60°
• Isosceles: 2 sides equal, base angles equal
• Scalene: all sides different, all angles different
• Right-angled: one angle = 90°

The exterior angle of a triangle equals the sum of the two non-adjacent interior angles.

Polygons

The sum of interior angles in a polygon with n sides = (n − 2) × 180°.

Examples:
• Triangle (3 sides): (3−2) × 180° = 180°
• Quadrilateral (4 sides): (4−2) × 180° = 360°
• Pentagon (5 sides): (5−2) × 180° = 540°
• Hexagon (6 sides): (6−2) × 180° = 720°

For a regular polygon, each interior angle = total ÷ number of sides.

questions.question: Two angles on a straight line are in the ratio 2:7. Find the smaller angle.
questions.explanation: Total = 180°. Parts = 2 + 7 = 9. Each part = 180 ÷ 9 = 20°. Smaller angle = 2 × 20 = 40°.

questions.question: A triangle has angles 47° and 68°. Find the third angle.
questions.explanation: Angles in a triangle add to 180°. Third angle = 180 − 47 − 68 = 65°.

questions.question: Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. One co-interior angle is 115°. What is the other co-interior angle?
questions.explanation: Co-interior angles are supplementary (add to 180°). So the other angle = 180° − 115° = 65°.

questions.question: Find the sum of interior angles in a hexagon
An exterior angle of a triangle is 130°. The two non-adjacent interior angles are equal. Find each of these angles.
questions.explanation: The exterior angle equals the sum of the two non-adjacent interior angles: 130°. Each angle = 130° ÷ 2 = 65°.
